company_name: McGraw Hill Financial, Inc. (S&P Global Inc.)

claims.claim_text: S&P Ratings Services holds a legally recognized Nationally Recognized Statistical Rating Organization (NRSRO) status - one of only three such designations (with Moody's and Fitch) - creating a government-mandated oligopoly for credit ratings. Regulatory requirements for pension funds, insurance companies, and banks to hold rated securities create inescapable demand for S&P ratings. S&P Ratings achieved 43.8% operating margin in 2014 and 46.3% in Q2 2014, demonstrating the durable pricing power this status confers.
evidence_quotes: In 2014, revenue increased 8%, adjusted operating profit grew 13% and the adjusted operating margin increased 190 basis points to 43.8%." (Q4 2014, Douglas Peterson)
During the quarter, revenue increased 11%, operating profit increased 15% and the operating margin increased 160 basis points to 46.3%." (Q2 2014, Douglas Peterson)
Demand for corporate debt ratings and bank loan ratings drove overall 2014 transaction revenue." (Q4 2014, Douglas Peterson)
claims.notes: NRSRO status is a legally protected designation that creates inescapable regulatory demand. 43-46% operating margins on the Ratings business confirm the pricing power of this cornered resource. Score 0.9 - strongest Cornered Resource claim in the chunk. Rule12-T: Q4 2014 annual call is primary.

claims.claim_text: S&P Dow Jones Indices operates a network business where AUM-based licensing fees grow as more investors adopt S&P indices. ETF AUM linked to S&P indices reached $832B in 2014 (up 25% YoY) and $719B in Q2 2014 (up 32% YoY). Mutual fund AUM crossed $1 trillion. More AUM tracking S&P indices → more ETF and mutual fund managers pay licensing fees → more index product development → more investor adoption: a classic network-reinforcing cycle.
evidence_quotes: ETF AUM associated with our indices increased 25% from the end of 2013 to $832 billion. Importantly, 15% of this growth was a result of new inflows." (Q4 2014, Douglas Peterson)
Mutual fund AUM associated with our indices reached another major milestone in 2014, surpassing the $1 trillion mark." (Q4 2014, Douglas Peterson)

ETF AUMs associated with our indices increased 32% to a record $719 billion from the end of the second quarter of 2013. Importantly, 11% of this increase was the result of new inflows." (Q2 2014, Douglas Peterson)

In 2014, every dollar of incremental revenue growth resulted in $1 of incremental adjusted operating profit. This resulted in a 2014 adjusted segment operating profit margin of 63.6%." (Q4 2014, Douglas Peterson)
claims.notes: 63.6% operating margin with every incremental dollar of revenue flowing directly to profit demonstrates the power of AUM-based licensing. The network effect is real: more indices attract more AUM, which attracts more licensing fees, which funds more index creation. Score 0.9.

claims.claim_text: S&P Capital IQ's enterprise desktop and data platform creates high switching costs through deep integration with customer workflows. The platform connects credit ratings (RatingsXpress), market intelligence, leveraged commentary and data, and now PresCenter (embedded Microsoft Office integration for pitchbook creation). 7-8% organic revenue growth and 4 consecutive quarters of adjusted profit growth through H1 2014 demonstrate growing customer stickiness.
evidence_quotes: S&P Capital IQ Desktop & Enterprise Solutions revenue increased 8%, and this was principally driven by an 11% increase in Desktop revenue." (Q4 2014, Douglas Peterson)
PresCenter is a productivity tool. It embeds Microsoft Office products within S&P Capital IQ Desktops to allow seamless links between data and presentations." (Q2 2014, Douglas Peterson)

After 2 years of investments, the business delivered adjusted operating margin improvement for the year. And the fourth quarter results were largely consistent with the full year results." (Q4 2014, Douglas Peterson)
claims.notes: Embedded workflows (PresCenter integrating S&P data into Microsoft Office pitchbooks) create genuine switching costs for financial professionals. However, competition from Bloomberg and FactSet limits the strength of the moat. Score 0.5.

claims.claim_text: Platts commodity price assessment business provides the legal market standard for physical commodity pricing globally. Price assessments for petroleum, natural gas, metals and agricultural commodities are used as contract reference prices by physical traders, banks, and government agencies. Platts grew high single-digit organically in 2014 despite dramatically falling oil prices, and doubled revenue over the preceding 5 years, demonstrating inelastic demand for its price benchmarking function.
evidence_quotes: For Platts, fourth quarter revenue was the strongest of the year, capping high single-digit organic revenue growth for 2014. With a backdrop of dramatically reduced oil prices, Petroleum, the largest category, delivered high single-digit growth in both the quarter and the year through increased demand for price assessments and market data subscription." (Q4 2014, Douglas Peterson)
By providing transparent pricing data and analysis, Platts assisted customers in managing commodity price volatility." (Q4 2014, Douglas Peterson)
Metals & Agriculture delivered the greatest rate of growth in 2014 at 34%." (Q4 2014, Douglas Peterson)
claims.notes: Platts price assessments are market standards embedded in physical commodity contracts - a form of legally referenced cornered resource. Revenue growth even with collapsing oil prices confirms inelastic demand. Score 0.7.

claims.claim_text: The secular trend toward passive investing (index funds and ETFs) provides a durable multi-decade CAGR tailwind for S&P Dow Jones Indices' AUM-based licensing revenue. Record ETF inflows of $331B in 2014 and growing European corporate bond market (expanding issuer base) support compound growth in both Indices and Ratings revenues. Management guided mid-single-digit revenue CAGR with 125+ basis points of annual margin expansion.
evidence_quotes: The ETF industry experienced record inflows of $331 billion in 2014. The trend towards passive investing continues, and S&P Dow Jones Indices, with its broad and innovative array of indices, is at the forefront of this trend." (Q4 2014, Douglas Peterson)

You can see a significant increase in our European customer base in the past 2 years. In order for European companies to meet their borrowing needs, they're increasingly turning to the capital markets. While quarterly issuance volumes ebb and flow, this is a very bullish long-term trend." (Q4 2014, Douglas Peterson)

Revenue from continuing operations has grown at a 10% compounded annual growth rate [over last 3 years]... We've achieved a compounded annual growth rate in earnings per share of 24%." (Q4 2014, Douglas Peterson)
claims.notes: Passive investing trend is a well-documented secular tailwind directly benefiting S&P Dow Jones Indices AUM-based fees. Record $331B ETF inflows in 2014 confirms the trend. Unlike commodity tailwinds, this benefits specifically S&P (not all index providers equally) given S&P 500 dominance. Score 0.7.

key_themes: S&P NRSRO legally mandated credit ratings oligopoly (Cornered Resource, 0.9)
S&P Dow Jones Indices $832B ETF AUM with 63.6% operating margin (Network Economies, 0.9)
Platts commodity price assessment as legal market standard (Cornered Resource, 0.7)
S&P Capital IQ enterprise workflow switching costs (Switching Costs, 0.5)
Passive investing secular trend as CAGR tailwind (Network Economies, 0.7)
extraction_summary.assessment: MHFI is the highest-quality competitive advantage profile in chunk_11. Two separate 0.9 confidence claims (NRSRO monopoly, index network effect) plus corroborating Platts and Capital IQ claims. 63.6% operating margin at S&P Dow Jones Indices is one of the highest in the S&P 500.
